mainmatter.p: We first show that the dependent product of intrinsic path objects \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } \mathopen {} \left ( -xx \right ) \mathclose {} ^{=} is a cocartesian family of path objects over the path object \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } \mathsf {Type}: given an equivalence H : G \cong G' of graphs and a reflexivity datum 1_{ \bullet } \colon \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } Gxx, we define H_* 1_{ \bullet } x : \equiv H \, x \, x \, 1_{ x }; the unit law holds definitionally.

Therefore, we may form the covariant sum \mathchoice { \textstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {} \left ( G: \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } \mathsf {Type} \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {} \left ( G: \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } \mathsf {Type} \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {} \left ( G: \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } \mathsf {Type} \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {} \left ( G: \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } \mathsf {Type} \right ) \mathclose {} } } \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } \mathopen {} \left ( Gxx \right ) \mathclose {} ^{=}, in which equivalences are given by homotopies of reflexivity data over graph equivalences.

frontmatter.title: The univalence principle for reflexive graphs

mainmatter.p: We may define a path object structure on the type of reflexive graphs with vertices in a given type A, so that an isomorphism from \mathopen {} \left ( G, 1_{ \bullet } \right ) \mathclose {} to \mathopen {} \left ( G', 1_{ \bullet } ' \right ) \mathclose {} is given by a pair of a pointwise equivalence of types H \colon \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x,y:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } Gxy \cong G'xy that preserves reflexivity data as witnessed by a dependent homotopy I \colon \mathopen {} \left ( \lambda x \mathpunct {.} H \, x \, x \, 1_{ x } \right ) \mathclose {} \sim 1_{ \bullet } '.
Let A be a path object; an A-indexed family B \colon A \to \mathsf {PathObj} of path objects is called a cocartesian family when it comes equipped with an opcleaving as defined below:

ol.li: a pushforward operation sending each p \colon x \cong _A y to some specified map p_* \colon Bx \to By;

a family \Phi _B \colon \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A,b:Bx \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A,b:Bx \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A,b:Bx \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A,b:Bx \right ) \mathclose {} } } \mathopen {} \left ( 1_{ x } \right ) \mathclose {} _*b \cong _{Bx} b witnessing the unit law that pushforward along the identity element 1_{ x } \colon x \cong _A x is the identity function.

frontmatter.title: Covariant sum of an cocartesian family of path objects

mainmatter.p: Let B \colon A \to \mathsf {PathObj} be an cocartesian family of path objects over a path object A. We define the covariant sum of B \colon A \to \mathsf {PathObj}, written \mathchoice { \textstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } {Bx}, to be the total path object of the displayed path object associated to B. In particular, we have defined \mathopen {} \left ( x,u \right ) \mathclose {} \cong _{ \mathchoice { \textstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \oint _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } {Bx}} \mathopen {} \left ( y,v \right ) \mathclose {} to be \mathchoice { \textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {} \left ( p : x \cong _A y \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {} \left ( p : x \cong _A y \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {} \left ( p : x \cong _A y \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ \mathopen {} \left ( p : x \cong _A y \right ) \mathclose {} } } p_*u \cong _{By} v.

Let B \colon A \to \mathsf {PathObj} be a family of path objects. We equip the dependent product \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } {Bx} with the structure of a path object, setting f \cong _{ \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } {Bx}} g : \equiv \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ \mathopen {} \left ( x:A \right ) \mathclose {} } } fx \cong _{Bx}gx and 1_{ f } : \equiv \lambda x \mathpunct {.} 1_{ fx }.
Univalence follows from the closure of families of contractible types under dependent products () via the distributivity of \prod over \sum.
The intrinsic path object on a type A, written A ^{=}, is defined by setting x \cong _{ A ^{=} } y : \equiv \mathopen {} \left ( x = y \right ) \mathclose {} and 1_{ x } : \equiv \mathsf {refl}.
That the reflexive graph defined above is univalent follows immediately by identification induction.
